You can choose to stay in a lodge or campsite at Yosemite, and the tour ends in Las Vegas. The tour lasts twelve days and covers 2,020 kilometres. It costs £525 per person including accommodation, car rental and flights, plus dinner. The second tour lasts nine days and costs £399 per person.

4.scene: 自我调节焦点理论领导力讲座

signpost.quote: Self-regulatory focus theory is a theory developed by Tori Higgins. He says that a person's focus at any given time is to either approach pleasure or avoid pain.
opts: Introducing the foundational concept before developing it further

signpost.quote: Promotion goals in different life situations emphasise achievement. Prevention goals are oriented towards the avoidance of punishment.
opts: Distinguishing the two types of goals before explaining how each affects behaviour

signpost.quote: Research has shown that the goals we are focusing on at a given time affect the way we think.
opts: Introducing empirical support for the theoretical distinction just made

signpost.quote: Now that I have talked about the two focuses and how they affect people, I want to look at the idea that the way leaders behave can affect the focus that followers adopt.
opts: Transitioning from individual psychology to the impact of leadership style

signpost.quote: In conclusion, it is important to understand that one focus is not necessarily better than the other one.
opts: Preventing misinterpretation before applying the theory to different professional contexts

mindmap.title: Two Types of Goals
items: Promotion goals: emphasise
— focus on ideal self, aspirations and gains
Prevention goals: driven by
— focus on obligations, avoiding mistakes

mindmap.title: Two Factors That Affect Focus
items: Chronic factor: connected to a person's basic
— their natural tendency
Situational factor: context matters — e.g. working with a
activates promotion; boss's project activates prevention

mindmap.title: Transformational vs Transactional Leaders
items: Transformational: focus on followers'
— passionate speech, definitive
Transactional: focus on clear
— emphasise consequences of making mistakes

mindmap.title: Applications
items: Designer needing
→ promotion focus is better
Surgeon needing caution and quality →
focus is more appropriate

dictation.speaker: LECTURER
parts: Self-regulatory focus theory says that a person's focus at any given time is to either approach
or avoid pain. These are two basic motivations that cause us to have different kinds of goals.
Promotion goals emphasise
. Prevention goals are oriented towards the avoidance of
Two factors affect which goals we are focusing on. First, a chronic factor connected to
. Second, a situational factor — the context we are in.
For example, we are more likely to have promotion goals when spending time with a
. In contrast, working on an important project for our boss makes us focus more on prevention goals.
When focusing on promotion goals, people consider their ideal self, their
, and gains. They feel more inspired to change.
When focusing on prevention goals, people think about their obligations to others. As a result, they experience more
and try to avoid situations where they could lose.
The way leaders behave can affect the focus that followers adopt. Transformational leaders focus on their followers'
. Their speech is passionate and conveys a definitive
Transactional leaders focus on clear
. They emphasise how a follower will be punished if his or her behaviour doesn't change — a prevention focus.
One focus is not necessarily better than the other. For a designer who needs
, a promotion focus is probably better. For a surgeon who needs accuracy and high quality, a
focus is more appropriate.

1.errorText: Edith Pargetter phones Mr Thorndyke the builder about work she needs doing in her flat. She heard about him from her friend May Hampton, for whom he did excellent work. Her flat is in West Park, Flat 4, at the back of the post office. The best time to call her is in the afternoon. She provides an itemised quote with separate prices. The most urgent job is fixing a cracked window in the living room. She also wants painting done over the cooker in the kitchen. In the garden, she wants a fence replaced.

dictation.speaker: GUIDE
parts: Welcome to Manham Port, where a thousand years of history are brought to life.
The port developed here because it's the highest
point of the Avon. A small port was already established here when, about 900 years ago, tin was discovered nearby.
By the end of the nineteenth century, lead, copper, manganese and arsenic were added to the
leaving Manham.
As demand grew, the smelting process required huge factory furnaces, but there was not enough coal locally — so the rocks containing minerals had to be shipped long
The Manham Trust organised local volunteers to remove
to find the original outlines of the installations. Professionals then reconstructed it.
Start with the visit to the copper mine. Travel on converted mining
and journey into the depths of the mountain. Please be warned that, like all mines, ours is very
Children under five and dogs should NOT be taken into the mine.
The next visit is to the village school — take a special look at our display of
, which is one of the largest in the world. We recommend you time your visit to coincide with a guided
Near the school is the beautiful old sailing ketch called 'The George'. Look out for the ship's
which was missing until only five years ago when it was dredged out of the
by a local fisherman.
Please take care going down the
if you wish to visit the lower deck — we don't recommend you allow young children to use them.

signpost.quote: However, as we'll see, the science of the small has some big implications affecting us in many ways.

dictation.speaker: LECTURER
parts: Nano means tiny — so nanotechnology is science and engineering on the scale of atoms and
. The idea is that by controlling and rearranging atoms, you can literally create anything.

Science-fiction books and movies have raised people's fears — scenarios such as self-replicating nano-robots that eventually take over the world. But most scientists would welcome a way to guard against
uses of such technology.
Thanks to nanotechnology, there could be a major breakthrough in the production of more durable
— virtually unbreakable, lighter and more pliable — leading to planes 50 times lighter than at present.
The computer industry will be able to shrink computer parts down to minute sizes. We could create a new generation of computers with a million times more
— about the size of a sugar cube.
The cost of
cells will be drastically reduced — harnessing this energy will be far more economical than at present.
Tiny airborne nano-robots could be programmed to rebuild the ozone layer, which could lessen the impact of global
on our planet.
Nanotechnology will allow us to remove
and other contaminants from water far more effectively.
If nanotechnology progresses as expected, you could build anything from the bottom up — you only use what you need, so there wouldn't be any
How we detect disease will change as tiny biosensors analyse
in minutes rather than days.
There's even speculation that nano-robots could be used to slow the ageing process, lengthening life
